name: Ekadashi Vrat
duration_note: The Ekadashi vrat spans 2 days: the fast on Ekadashi and the Parana (fast-breaking) on Dwadashi morning

recurrence.repeat_note: Ideally observed every Ekadashi (24 times a year). Starting with one Ekadashi per month (12 times) is the most common vow.

sankalpa_text_template: ममात्मनः सर्वपापक्षयपूर्वकदीर्घायुर्बलपुष्टिनैरुज्यैश्वर्याभिवृद्ध्यर्थं... [user fills name, gotra, location] ...एकादश्यां उपवासं करिष्ये ।
sankalpa_guide: Stand facing east. Hold water in your cupped hands. Say your name, your father's name (or gotra if known), and your location. Then recite the sankalpa. Release the water into a vessel or onto the earth.

preparation: On Dashami (day before Ekadashi): eat only once, avoid beans, lentils, and meat. Eat before sunset.
Prepare a clean space for Vishnu puja — wash the idol or image
Gather puja materials (Tulsi leaves, yellow flowers, incense, ghee lamp, bell)
Inform family members of your vrat — their cooperation helps
Charge your phone to ensure the Parana alarm doesn't miss

materials.name: Tulsi leaves (fresh)
materials.purpose: Primary offering to Vishnu — the most essential item
materials.substitute: Dried Tulsi if fresh unavailable, but fresh is far superior

materials.name: Bell (ghanta)
materials.purpose: To announce the puja to the deity

materials.name: Fruits
materials.purpose: Prasad offering and food during the fast

materials.name: Sesame seeds (til)
materials.purpose: For Parana on Dwadashi — auspicious to eat when breaking fast

steps.title: Wake up before sunrise
steps.action: Set an alarm for Brahma Muhurta (app alarm fires automatically). Wake up, drink a glass of water, and go to the bathroom.
steps.note: If you cannot wake up at Brahma Muhurta, waking up at sunrise is acceptable.

steps.title: Bathe and purify
steps.action: Take a full bath. If not possible, wash hands, feet, and face at minimum. Wear clean clothes — light colors preferred (white or yellow).
steps.note: Physical purity is the outer gate to inner purity.

steps.title: Take the Sankalpa
steps.action: Stand facing east. Hold water in cupped hands. Say your name and location, then recite the Ekadashi Vrat Sankalpa (shown on screen). Release the water.
steps.note: The sankalpa is the formal beginning of the vrat. It makes your intention concrete and conscious.

steps.title: Perform Vishnu Puja
steps.action: Light the ghee lamp and incense. Offer Tulsi leaves and flowers to Vishnu's image. Ring the bell. Chant the 8 names of Vishnu (Ashtottara Sata Namashtaka).
steps.note: Tulsi is essential. Without Tulsi, Vishnu puja is incomplete.

steps.title: Spend the day in spiritual activities
steps.action: Throughout the day: chant 'Om Namo Narayanaya' or 'Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya'. Read the Mahatmya of today's Ekadashi. Avoid TV and social media where possible.
steps.note: Even 10 minutes of chanting counts. Quality of attention matters more than quantity.

steps.title: Pradosha Puja (Evening)
steps.action: At sunset, light another ghee lamp before Vishnu. Offer sandalwood paste (chandan). Chant the 8 names again. Perform aarti.

steps.title: Night vigil (optional but meritorious)
steps.action: Stay awake through the night in Vishnu's name. Sing bhajans, listen to Vishnu Sahasranama, or read Bhagavad Gita. Even staying awake till midnight counts.
steps.note: Jagran (night vigil) on Ekadashi is said to give merit equivalent to performing the Ashwamedha yajna.

steps.title: Parana — Breaking the fast (next morning, Dwadashi)
steps.action: App alarm fires at the Parana start time. Chant the Parana Shloka. First eat a few Tulsi leaves dissolved in water. Then eat sattvic food — fruit, milk, or grain (rice is allowed on Dwadashi).
steps.note: Breaking the fast outside the Parana window reduces the vrat's merit. The app will alert you when the window opens and when it closes.

steps.title: Kshama Prarthana — Forgiveness
steps.action: After Parana, sit quietly and chant the Kshama Prarthana for any steps you missed or mistakes you made during the vrat.
steps.note: This step completes the vrat regardless of imperfections. God accepts sincere effort, not perfect ritual.

mantras_explained.why_this_mantra: The Sankalpa is a formal declaration to God and to yourself: 'I am doing this consciously, with a specific intention.' It creates a container for the entire day. Without sankalpa, the fast is just not eating — with sankalpa, it becomes a vrat (vow) that carries spiritual weight.
mantras_explained.how_to_pronounce: Speak slowly and clearly. The words matter less than the intention behind them. If you mispronounce, it is forgiven — God listens to the heart, not just the lips.
mantras_explained.what_to_visualize: See yourself standing before Lord Vishnu, who is seated on Ananta Shesha in the cosmic ocean. He is looking at you with infinite patience and love. You are making a promise to Him.

mantras_explained.why_this_mantra: Each of these 8 names of Vishnu describes a different quality of God: the all-pervading, the liberator, the indweller, the refuge, the bearer of Lakshmi, the lord of the senses, the lotus-navel, the one bound by Yashoda's love. Chanting all 8 is like greeting God from 8 different angles — each name activates a different aspect of His grace.
mantras_explained.how_to_pronounce: Om Vishna-ve na-mah | Om Ja-nar-da-na-ya na-mah | Om Vaa-su-de-va-ya na-mah | Om Naa-raa-ya-na-ya na-mah | Om Shree-dha-ra-ya na-mah | Om Hri-shee-ke-sha-ya na-mah | Om Pad-ma-naa-bha-ya na-mah | Om Daa-mo-da-ra-ya na-mah
mantras_explained.what_to_visualize: See the four-armed Vishnu in his Vaikuntha form — holding the Shankha (conch), Chakra (discus), Gada (mace), and Padma (lotus). He is smiling at you.

mantras_explained.why_this_mantra: The Parana shloka is gratitude. It says: 'I completed this vrat imperfectly, as a human must. Please give me the full fruit of it anyway.' It is the closing handshake that seals the vrat.
mantras_explained.how_to_pronounce: Aj-nyaa-naat kri-ta-paa-paa-naam jnyaa-naad vaa su-ra-pun-ga-va | Paa-ra-nam ku-ru de-ve-sha e-kaa-da-shyaah pha-lam ma-ma ||
mantras_explained.what_to_visualize: See yourself completing the journey. The fast is over. Lord Vishnu is receiving your offering with both hands.

alarms.label: Brahma Muhurta — Wake up for Ekadashi
alarms.notification_message: Ekadashi Brahma Muhurta — Wake up, bathe, and take Sankalpa

alarms.label: Pradosha Puja Time
alarms.notification_message: Pradosha time — Light the lamp and offer Tulsi to Vishnu

alarms.label: Parana Window Opens — Break your fast now
alarms.notification_message: Parana window open! Chant the Parana Shloka and break your Ekadashi fast
alarms.active_dates_note: This alarm fires on Dwadashi morning, not Ekadashi morning — computed by the app

alarms.label: Parana window closing in 15 minutes
alarms.notification_message: Parana window closes in 15 minutes — break your fast now if you haven't yet
alarms.active_dates_note: This alarm fires on Dwadashi morning, approximately 3 hrs 45 min after sunrise

materials_disposal.disposal_method: Leave on the altar or place at the base of the Tulsi plant
materials_disposal.note: Do not throw in the trash — Tulsi is sacred

materials_disposal.disposal_method: Immerse in flowing water or bury in the earth
Scatter in the garden or near a tree
Dispose respectfully in earth or compost
materials_disposal.note: Do not throw in the regular trash

materials_disposal.disposal_method: Distribute to family members and eat yourself
materials_disposal.note: Ekadashi prasad — fruit offered to Vishnu — is auspicious and should be consumed, not discarded

after_completion.prasad_distribution: Distribute fruit prasad to all family members. If sesame was offered, it can be mixed with water and consumed during Parana.
next_steps: Mark this Ekadashi as completed in the app
On Dwadashi, donate food or money to Brahmins or the needy — Dwadashi is the ideal day for Annadaan
The next Ekadashi is in approximately 14–15 days — the app will remind you 2 days before

udyapan.description: Ekadashi vrat does not require a formal Udyapan. Each Ekadashi is complete in itself. However, if you vowed to observe a specific number of Ekadashis (e.g. 12), a simple thanks-giving puja on the last one is appropriate.
